channel.title: EO Glossary
channel.description: Community thesaurus of terms and definitions for Earth Observation sciences, maintained by CEOS and the EC Knowledge Centre for Earth Observation (KCEO).
channel.dc:creator: CEOS & EC KCEO

item.title: Time of Year
item.description: Time of the year at which the variable is observed or simulated by a model.

item.title: Timeliness
item.description: Period between the moment of requesting information and the moment of availability of information.

item.title: Traceability
item.description: Property of a measurement result whereby the result can be related to a reference through a documented unbroken chain of calibrations each contributing to the measurement uncertainty.

item.title: Trait
item.description: Characteristic belonging to an entity and referenced by an identifier.

item.title: Uncertainty
item.description: Parameter, associated with the result of a measurement, that characterises the dispersion of the values that could reasonably be attributed to the measurand.

item.title: Update Frequency
item.description: Frequency at which the product is available to users.

item.title: User
item.description: Entity (person, organization, institution, etc.) that is requesting data or information with certain characteristics described by needs and/or requirements.

item.title: User Interface
item.description: Set of all the components of an interactive system that provide information and controls for the user to accomplish specific tasks with the interactive system.

item.title: Validation
item.description: Process of assessing, by independent means, the quality of the data products derived from the system outputs.

item.title: Value
item.description: Element of a type domain.

item.title: Verification
item.description: Provision of objective evidence that a given item fulfils specified requirements.

item.title: Version Control
item.description: Method of working with collections, similar to version control systems used in traditional software development but optimized to allow better processing of data and collaboration in data analytics and research contexts.

item.title: Vertical Levels
item.description: Levels of a vertical discretization, e.g. pressure levels in an atmospheric model reanalysis or height levels in a forest biomass EO product.

item.title: Sample
item.description: Subset of one or more entities.

item.title: Sensor
item.description: Device or instrument suited (through physical/chemical interaction) to measure one or more properties of a Phenomenon and thus collect factual/objective data.

item.title: Source Data
item.description: Data required for production of an EO Collection (e.g., unenhanced satellite mission data) as well as further processed Level-1 data, or any other pre-processed format meeting program requirements.

item.title: Spatial Completeness
item.description: Presence or absence of gaps, which are missing values in an otherwise continuous spatial data series.

item.title: Spatial Consistency
item.description: Condition where the spatial statistical properties of the data depend only on the underlying physical processes and not on other factors such as fusing different products or sensors.

item.title: Spatial Extent
item.description: Zone or region of space described by a geographic identifier in the form of a label or code, or by a bounding box, representing the maximum spatial boundary within which the user is requesting data.

item.title: Spatial Reporting Unit
item.description: Smallest spatial object of interest that may be used for reporting and for which the information should be aggregated.

item.title: Spectral Band
item.description: Part of the electromagnetic spectrum of specific wavelengths, in remote sensing usually described by central wavelength and bandwidth.

item.title: Spectral Bandwidth
item.description: Range of the Spectral Band.

item.title: Spectral Resolution
item.description: Measure of the ability to resolve features in the electromagnetic spectrum.

item.title: Stability
item.description: Maximum acceptable change in Uncertainty per decade.

item.title: Standard Uncertainty
item.description: Standard deviation of the probability distribution describing the spread of possible values in quantitative/continuous data.

item.title: Steward
item.description: Entity responsible for the safe custody, transport, storage of the data and implementation of business rules.

item.title: Temporal Consistency
item.description: Condition where the temporal statistical properties of the sample depend only on the underlying physical processes and not on other factors such as fusing different products or sensors.

item.title: Temporal Extent
item.description: Period during which data was collected, observations were made, or for which the model was run.

item.title: Temporal Reporting Period
item.description: Time period used for reporting and for which the information should be aggregated from the native temporal resolution.

item.title: Temporal Resolution
item.description: Observation or model output representing regular intervals, specifying the length of the interval that determines the smallest event or process that can be resolved.

item.title: Temporal Revisit
item.description: Interval between successive observations.

item.title: Thematic Resolution
item.description: Level of categorical detail of information expressed by the number of classes.

item.title: Thematic Uncertainty
item.description: Uncertainty associated with the method of classification.

item.title: Time of Day
item.description: Time of the day at which the variable or parameter is observed or simulated by a model.

item.title: Period
item.description: Particular era or span of time.

item.title: Period Identifier
item.description: Temporal reference in the form of a label or code that identifies a Period.

item.title: Phenomenon
item.description: Entity with at least one Property referenced by an identifier.

item.title: Place
item.description: Part of any space referenced by an identifier.

item.title: Policy
item.description: Deliberate system of guidelines to guide decisions and achieve rational outcomes, implemented as a procedure or protocol and typically promulgated through official written documents.

item.title: Policy Cycle
item.description: Well-established concept typically taught as the rational model of policy decision-making, representing an idealised view of the policy process.

item.title: Policy File
item.description: Policy documents that represent the official written reference for a given Policy.

item.title: Policy Making
item.description: Process by which governments translate their political vision into programmes and actions to deliver outcomes — desired change in the real world.

item.title: Policy Milestone
item.description: Markers of significant progress in addressing specific issues, or crucial decision points where significant choices are made within a policy process.

item.title: Policy Objective
item.description: Desired outcome that policymakers wish to achieve.

item.title: Policy Target
item.description: Specific level or rate set for the Policy Objective.

item.title: Position
item.description: Place referenced by a single set of coordinates within a coordinate reference system.

item.title: Preservation
item.description: Processes and operations used to ensure data technical and intellectual survival through time.

item.title: Process
item.description: Series of activities that interact to produce a result.

item.title: Product
item.description: Electronic data package distributable to users; content is derived from instrument data via processing involving ancillary and auxiliary data.

item.title: Property
item.description: Observable Trait.

item.title: Provisional Product
item.description: Products in a research and development phase not yet published into a standardized collection.

item.title: Quality Indicator
item.description: Indicator providing sufficient information to allow all users to readily evaluate the fitness for purpose of the data or derived product.

item.title: Quantity
item.description: Property having a magnitude that can be expressed as a number from a continuous and contiguous range and a reference.

item.title: Reference
item.description: Phenomenon whose values for specific properties are known and understood with an Uncertainty significantly lower than that of the Observation with which it is compared.

item.title: Remote Sensing
item.description: Type of Observation performed at a significant distance from a Phenomenon.

item.title: Replicability
item.description: Property enabling other researchers to conduct an independent study with different data and similar but not identical methods, yet arriving at results that confirm the original study's hypothesis.

item.title: Representativeness
item.description: Quality or characteristic of a measurement or observation to accurately represent a specific geographic location, time period, or environmental condition.

item.title: Reproducibility
item.description: Concerned with the validity of the results of a particular study, enabling readers to check whether results have been manipulated by reproducing exactly the same study using the same data and methods.

item.title: Hydrosphere
item.description: The masses of liquid water, such as oceans, lakes, and rivers.

item.title: Impact
item.description: Regarded long-term according to X. However, in the Horizon Europe Missions terminology, regarded short-term.

item.title: In-Situ Observation
item.description: Observations performed in the same Place where a Phenomenon occurs, normally without isolating it from other systems or altering its pre-Observation state.

item.title: Information
item.description: The result of organisation, interpretation, categorisation, classification, or some other form of processing of Data attaching to it a certain meaning that can be understood by the addressee.

item.title: Instrument Data
item.description: Data specifically associated with the instrument, either because it was generated by the instrument or included in data packets identified with that instrument.

item.title: Laboratory Observation
item.description: Usually in-situ observations in which the Object or Phenomenon is isolated from other systems or altered in its original conditions or environment.

item.title: Latency
item.description: Period between the end of sensing of a Phenomenon to the beginning of availability of a specific product.

item.title: Lead Time
item.description: Minimum time of interval between an Observation being tasked and being performed.

item.title: Lithosphere
item.description: The solid, inanimate outer layer of the Geosphere. For the purposes of Earth Observation, includes soils ('pedosphere').

item.title: Location
item.description: Particular Place referenced by an identifier.

item.title: Location Error
item.description: Measure of the agreement between the represented Location of an Object and the true Location.

item.title: Long Term Preservation
item.description: The act of maintaining information in a correct and independently understandable form over the long term.

item.title: Measurand
item.description: Particular Quantity subject to Measurement.

item.title: Measurement
item.description: Observation of a Quantity.

item.title: Measurement Uncertainty
item.description: Uncertainty associated with the method of Measurement.

item.title: Metadata
item.description: Data describing other data, providing an understanding of the content and utility of a product or collection.

item.title: Minimum Mapping Unit
item.description: The area of the smallest Feature that is still represented on a map.

item.title: Minimum Mapping Width
item.description: The width of the smallest linear Feature that is still represented on a map.

item.title: Model
item.description: An, often numeric, theoretical (or otherwise abstract) representation of an Entity intended to generate Data describing one or more of its traits.

item.title: Near Real Time Data
item.description: Data available for use with a specified (small and application dependent) latency, typically 3 hours for meteorological applications.

item.title: Object
item.description: Entity with a well-defined boundary and identity that encapsulates state and behaviour.

item.title: Observation
item.description: Act of determining the Value of a Property by interacting in a reproducible way with the Phenomenon using a Sensor.

item.title: Obsolete Product or Collection
item.description: Products or collections which have been superseded by later versions of a product or collection.

item.title: Path
item.description: Line defined in an algorithmic way using at least two positions (can be open-ended). Alternative: line connecting two positions in an algorithmic way (limited by end points).

item.title: Earth Observation
item.description: Science domain dealing with technologies and methods of collecting and analyzing observations about the different Earth spheres.

item.title: Earth Observation Based Output
item.description: Higher-level processing and integration output (indicative level 4 and beyond) that goes beyond basic data processing, often designed for policy or decision-making needs.

item.title: Earth Observation Product
item.description: Direct output from standardized satellite data processing chains (indicative levels 2 and 3), representing fundamental geophysical and biophysical variables.

item.title: Earth Spheres
item.description: Spheres dividing planet Earth into subsystems, which are collectives of (physical) matter sharing specific traits.

item.title: Entity
item.description: Something that has separate and distinct existence and objective or conceptual reality.

item.title: EO Space Data
item.description: Earth Observation data generated by spaceborne missions or instruments owned by public or private organizations.

item.title: Expanded Uncertainty
item.description: Standard Uncertainty multiplied by a coverage factor k, chosen to obtain a desired level of confidence.

item.title: Feature
item.description: Abstraction of real-world phenomena.

item.title: Final Product Version
item.description: Product within an EO Collection that is not expected to be updated.

item.title: Forecast Range
item.description: The forecast period.

item.title: Geocoding
item.description: Translation of one form of Location into another.

item.title: Geographic Coordinate Reference System
item.description: Coordinate Reference System (CRS) that has a geodetic Reference frame and an ellipsoidal coordinate system.

item.title: Geographic Data
item.description: Data with implicit or explicit reference to a Location relative to the Earth.

item.title: Geographic Grid
item.description: Regular grid based on a Geographic Coordinate Reference System (CRS).

item.title: Geographic Identifier
item.description: Spatial reference in the form of a label or code that identifies a Location.

item.title: Geolocating
item.description: Geopositioning of an Object using a Physical Sensor Model or a True Replacement Model.

item.title: Geolocation Information
item.description: Information used to determine geographic Location.

item.title: Geopositioning
item.description: Determination of the geographic Position of a point (0D) or linear (1D) Feature.

item.title: Georectifying
item.description: Correcting for positional displacement with respect to the surface of the Earth.

item.title: Geospatial Data
item.description: Data consisting of, derived from, or relating to information that is directly linked to specific geographical locations.

item.title: Geosphere
item.description: The masses of minerals that constitute planet Earth.

item.title: Granule
item.description: The smallest aggregation of data which is independently managed (i.e. described, inventoried, retrievable).

item.title: Grid
item.description: Network composed of two or more sets of curves in which the members of each set intersect the members of the other sets in an algorithmic way.

item.title: Accuracy
item.description: Proximity of Measurement results to the accepted Value; precision is the degree to which repeated or reproducible measurements under unchanged conditions show the same results.

item.title: Ancillary Data
item.description: Data acquired on the same platform but not obtained from the main Sensor itself (usually provided as part of Level 0 Data), with the primary purpose of serving the processing of main instrument Data.

item.title: Anthroposphere
item.description: One of the Earth spheres consisting predominantly of matter processed by humans, such as construction wood, bricks, concrete, asphalt, glass, or plastics.

item.title: Archive
item.description: System that stores data products, guaranteeing their preservation for future use, including all operations to identify, store and retrieve the data and ensure their integrity.

item.title: Area of Interest
item.description: Zone in 2D or 3D for which Information or Data is requested. It can be discontinuous in space, consisting of the union of many separate zones.

item.title: Atmosphere
item.description: Layer of gases, commonly known as air, including suspended liquid and solid particles known as aerosols (incl. dust, clouds, snow and ice).

item.title: Auxiliary Data
item.description: Data that enhance the processing and utilisation of main Sensor Data, usually not captured by the same Data collection Process or on the same platform as the main Sensor Data.

item.title: Backup
item.description: General practice of making copies of current baseline and derived products of EO Collections at specified time intervals, storing those copies in a manner that will not affect the data if the primary distribution environment is compromised.

item.title: Band Central Wavelength
item.description: Single wavelength Value within the sensitivity interval of a spectroradiometric Sensor which represents the respective band. It could be the mean, median, maximum sensitivity, or any other reasonable representative Value.

item.title: Baseline
item.description: Reference Period, time or measure against which Information is assessed or compared.

item.title: Biosphere
item.description: Masses of living organisms, such as vegetation and animals, including dead but still connected parts such as roots, trunks or branches.

item.title: Business Continuity
item.description: An organisation's readiness to continue functioning during times of disruption.

item.title: Calibration
item.description: Process of quantitatively defining a system's (e.g. a Sensor or Model's) response to known, controlled inputs produced by references. In Remote Sensing, implies the collection of pre-flight and in-flight calibration measurements and in-situ Data.

item.title: Catalogue
item.description: System that provides users with discovery of information on which EO products can be obtained.

item.title: Cell
item.description: Spatial, spatio-temporal zone or temporal unit of geometry with dimensionality greater than 0, associated with a zone.

item.title: Characteristic
item.description: Distinguishing Feature of an object or entity, which can be inherent or assigned, and qualitative or quantitative.

item.title: Classification System
item.description: Nomenclature used to classify Information into categorical thematic classes (e.g., EUNIS, MAES ecosystems, Corine Land Cover, LCCS).

item.title: Classification System Levels
item.description: Levels in a hierarchical Classification System.

item.title: Climate Projection
item.description: Simulated response of the climate system to a scenario of future emission or concentration of greenhouse gases (GHGs) and aerosols, generally derived using climate models.

item.title: Collection
item.description: Group of products that have been processed using a consistent radiometric calibration, geometric registration base, data/metadata format, and version of processing algorithms.

item.title: Collection Update
item.description: Minor revisions to a relatively small number of products within a collection for the purpose of correcting errors and maintaining consistent quality.

item.title: Collection Upgrade
item.description: New and improved major version of all products within a collection due to comprehensive reprocessing.

item.title: Confidence Interval
item.description: Probability that a Quantity lies in a given interval, conditioned on the measuring or modelling assumptions.

item.title: Copernicus Service Provider
item.description: Institutional body, organisation or programme that provides reliable, trusted EO Information and has the financial resources to sustain the provision.

item.title: Coverage Interval
item.description: Range or specific subset of values associated with a coverage dataset.

item.title: Cryosphere
item.description: Masses of frozen water, such as sea ice, ice shields, glaciers, and snow.

item.title: Custodian
item.description: Entity responsible for the safe custody, transport, storage of the data and implementation of business rules.

item.title: Data
item.description: Value and possibly Uncertainty of a Trait of a specific Entity.

item.title: Data Access
item.description: Service to disseminate Data.

item.title: Data Format
item.description: Structured Data that is machine readable and can be automatically read and processed by a computer, such as HDF, NetCDF, CSV, JSON, XML, etc.

item.title: Data Licencing
item.description: Legal instrument by which a copyright holder may grant rights over the protected work.

item.title: Decommissioned Product
item.description: Products and collections that have been superseded by new versions and are no longer available.

item.title: Deprecated Product
item.description: Products and collections that have been superseded by a newer version and are still available, but whose use is discouraged since they will be decommissioned in the future.

item.title: Derivative Product
item.description: Product additionally processed from baseline or unenhanced mission data.

item.title: Dispose
item.description: Action of permanently and irrecoverably removing all copies of data held by an organization.

item.title: Duration
item.description: Non-negative interval Quantity of time equal to the difference between the final and initial instants of a time interval.

item.title: Dwell Time
item.description: Time that an antenna beam spends on a target.
